experiment: Ref PAI forward dump + nano-side per-block diff
batch: iter 5989 global batch, samples 28..31 (rank-7 mbs0 on DP=2 EP=4)
diagnosis: Block 0 (DENSE layer, not MoE) already diverges from ref at bf16-ULP level (L_inf=0.125 on activations of magnitude up to 16). Diff accumulates monotonically through 9 blocks to rel_mean=2.1%. Final loss diff is 0.5% (0.014 nat on 3.07).
root_cause_confirmed: Different bf16 matmul kernel implementations between nano (PyTorch F.linear + separate RMSNorm) and ref (TE's fused LayerNormLinear). Same math, different tiling/accumulation order in bf16 produces 1-2 ULP per-element diff per block. Compounds through 9 layers.
to_fully_close_gap: Replace nano's (RMSNorm + F.linear) with TE's fused LayerNormLinear
Or replace nano's SwiGLU MLP with TE's fused bias_swiglu
Or train nano in fp32 (slow but mathematically identical)
